title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Wed Apr 29, 2026
summary: Falcon Heavy returned to flight on April 29 after an 18-month gap, launching the ViaSat-3 F3 satellite (6.6 t) to geosynchronous transfer orbit from LC-39A at 14:13 UTC — the 12th-ever Falcon Heavy mission and SpaceX's 52nd orbital flight of 2026. Side boosters B1072 and B1075 simultaneously landed at LZ-2 and newly inaugurated LZ-40, marking the 604th and 605th cumulative Falcon booster recoveries. Two prior weather scrubs on April 27 and 28 delayed the mission; a Motley Fool IPO timeline published April 27 set the SpaceX Nasdaq debut window at June 18–30 ($75B raise, ~$1.75T valuation, 30% retail allocation). KPIs, timeline, and meta updated to reflect the 52nd orbital launch and 605 total booster recoveries.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Tue Apr 28, 2026
summary: SpaceX confirmed a second consecutive scrub for the Falcon Heavy ViaSat-3 F3 mission on April 28, moving the next attempt to Wednesday April 29 at 10:13 a.m.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Apr 27, 2026
summary: Falcon Heavy ViaSat-3 F3 Scrubbed (Weather); Motley Fool: SpaceX IPO Window June 18–30, $75B Raise.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Sun Apr 26, 2026
summary: Starlink 17-16 lifted off at 14:37 UTC from Vandenberg SLC-4E — SpaceX's 51st orbital launch of 2026 and 50th Falcon 9 flight of the year. Booster B1088 completed its 15th flight, landing on OCISLY for the 193rd time, marking the 603rd cumulative Falcon booster recovery in SpaceX history. ARK Invest published a comprehensive SpaceX IPO valuation analysis on the same day, arguing the $1.75 trillion target is not the ceiling: citing Starlink's projected $20B+ 2026 revenue, Starship's sub-$100/kg cost trajectory, and orbital AI compute infrastructure, with SpaceX's 2025 record of 165 missions (~85% of all global orbital launches) as a demand baseline. KPIs updated to reflect 51 orbital launches and 603 booster recoveries.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Sat Apr 25, 2026
summary: Starlink 17-16 (B1088, 15th flight, 25 Starlink V2 Mini sats, OCISLY recovery) was scrubbed from its April 25 window at Vandenberg SLC-4E and rescheduled to April 26, pushing SpaceX's 51st orbital launch of 2026 by one day. On the same day, Motley Fool published 'The SpaceX IPO Could Make Millionaires' — a retail investor guide to the planned $75B June 2026 Nasdaq debut at a $1.75–$2T valuation, noting the unprecedented 1/3 retail allocation and diversified revenue across Starlink, xAI, and social infrastructure. The piece marks four consecutive days of major financial media IPO coverage following Bloomberg's April 24 competitive moat analysis.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Apr 24, 2026
summary: Bloomberg published a competitive moat analysis on April 24 confirming SpaceX's IPO is now targeting above $2 trillion — up from $1.75T at the April 1 S-1 filing. Starlink's projected 2026 revenue of $15.9–24B (Quilty Space / Bloomberg), the Golden Dome defense software consortium contract (April 22), a $178.5M SDA missile-tracking satellite order, and the $60B Cursor AI acquisition option (April 23) are cited as compounding barriers to competition. At $2T+, SpaceX's June 2026 Nasdaq IPO targeting a $75B raise would dwarf Saudi Aramco's 2019 record. KPI updated to reflect $2T+ valuation target.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Thu Apr 23, 2026
summary: SpaceX announced a $60 billion option to acquire Anysphere, makers of the Cursor AI coding assistant, on April 23, 2026 — with a $10B collaborative alternative also on the table. The deal pairs Cursor's AI-assisted development tool with SpaceX's Colossus supercomputer cluster (1M H100 equivalents), positioning SpaceX as an AI company ahead of its planned June 2026 Nasdaq IPO. KPIs updated: 2026 orbital launches now at 50 (50th: Starlink 17-14, April 22). A new contested claim added on the strategic rationale for the Cursor acquisition.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Wed Apr 22, 2026
summary: Starlink 17-14 Launches from Vandenberg — B1100 8th Flight on OCISLY; SpaceX's 50th Orbital Launch of 2026.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Tue Apr 21, 2026
summary: GPS III SV-10 'Hedy Lamarr' Launches — Final GPS III Satellite Deployed; JRTI Retires from Falcon Duty.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: GPS III SV-10 'Hedy Lamarr' — the 10th and final satellite completing the advanced GPS III constellation for the U.S. Space Force — was scrubbed from its April 20 window at SLC-40, Cape Canaveral and rescheduled for April 21 at 2:53 a.m. EDT. Falcon 9 booster B1095 (7th flight) will attempt an Atlantic recovery on drone ship JRTI; the mission would be SpaceX's 49th orbital launch of 2026. The satellite is named for actress Hedy Lamarr, co-inventor of frequency-hopping spread spectrum — the foundational technology underlying modern GPS. Starship IFT-12 remains on track for May 2026; SpaceX IPO S-1 process continues toward a June 2026 Nasdaq debut.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Sun Apr 19, 2026
summary: SpaceX achieved its historic 600th cumulative Falcon booster landing on April 19 when B1097 touched down on drone ship OCISLY after delivering 25 Starlink V2 Mini satellites (Starlink 17-22), bringing 2026's orbital launch total to 48. The milestone comes 10 years and 4 months after the first booster landing in December 2015. On April 17, SpaceX accelerated employee stock option vesting ahead of its planned June 2026 Nasdaq IPO, while Space.com and TeslaOracle published comprehensive coverage confirming both IFT-12 Starship vehicles (Booster 19 and Ship 39) are cleared for stacking after their successful static fires on April 15-16. KPIs updated to reflect 600 landings and 48 launches; IPO claims updated with vesting acceleration.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: Booster 19 made history on April 16 by firing all 33 Raptor 3 engines for a full 15-second static fire at Pad 2, Starbase — the most powerful rocket test in SpaceX history and the final ground milestone before IFT-12 in May 2026. Ship 39 completed its 6-engine 60-second Raptor 3 static fire on April 15, clearing both V3 vehicles for stacking. SpaceX executed back-to-back launches 19 hours apart: Starlink 10-24 from Cape Canaveral (Apr 14, 29 sats, 1,000th Starlink satellite of 2026, B1080 26th = 598th recovery) and Starlink 17-27 from Vandenberg (Apr 15, 25 sats, B1082 21st = 599th recovery). Cygnus NG-24 S.S. Steven R. Nagel was captured and berthed at the ISS on April 13. Starlink 17-22 scrubbed April 18 — rescheduled for April 19 as the milestone 600th Falcon booster landing attempt.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Apr 17, 2026
summary: SpaceX Accelerates Employee IPO Vesting; IFT-12 Vehicles Clear for Stacking After Dual Static Fires.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: Historic: Booster 19 Fires All 33 Raptor 3 Engines — IFT-12 Final Ground Milestone Cleared.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched Starlink Group 17-27 at 9:29:49 p.m. Ship 39 (V3 Starship) Completes First-Ever 6-Engine Raptor 3 Static Fire at Starbase.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Tue Apr 14, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched Starlink Group 10-24 on April 14, 2026, at 5:23 a.m.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Apr 13, 2026
summary: Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL spacecraft, the S.S.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Northrop Grumman's Cygnus NG-24 cargo spacecraft, named the S.S. Booster 19 + Ship 39 Photos Released — SpaceX Ready for Historic 33-Raptor-3 Static Fire Ahead of IFT-12.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched Starlink Group 17-21 on April 10, 2026, from Space Launch Complex 4 East (SLC-4E) at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, with a liftoff window between 7:39 and 11:39 p.m.

title: SpaceX History Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: SpaceX successfully launched two Falcon 9 missions since the last update: Starlink 10-58 on April 2 (B1085's 15th flight, 29 satellites from Cape Canaveral) and the long-awaited Starlink 17-35 on April 6 from Vandenberg — marking booster B1103's debut flight after five consecutive scrubs, with recovery on OCISLY. These missions push SpaceX's 2026 launch total to 42 and all-time booster recoveries to 595. On April 3, Elon Musk confirmed Starship IFT-12 is 4–6 weeks away, officially shifting the V3 maiden voyage target to May 2026 — the third schedule slip this year. As of April 9, SpaceX has completed installation of all 33 Raptor 3 engines on Super Heavy Booster 19, with a full-duration 33-engine static fire expected within days alongside a parallel Ship 39 static fire at the Massey test site.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched 25 Starlink internet satellites aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 4 East (SLC-4E) at Vandenberg Space Force Base on April 8, 2026, liftoff at approximately 7:49 p.m.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: Industry coverage on April 7, 2026 highlighted the significance of SpaceX's previous night's B1103 debut launch.

title: SpaceX History Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: SpaceX formally entered the public markets process on April 1, 2026, filing a confidential IPO registration statement with the SEC — codenamed 'Project Apex' — targeting a ~$75 billion raise at a ~$1.75 trillion valuation in what would be the largest IPO in U.S. history, with a June 2026 Nasdaq listing and Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs as lead underwriters. Starship IFT-12, the debut of the V3 architecture with Raptor 3 engines, slipped from its late-April target to approximately May 2026 as Booster 19 awaited reinstallation of its remaining 23 engines before a full 33-engine static fire. The Starlink 17-35 rideshare mission (25 satellites, B1103 debut flight) suffered four consecutive scrubs through April 5 and is now targeting April 6 from Vandenberg.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: Elon Musk Confirms Starship IFT-12 Is 4–6 Weeks Away — Third Slip Targets May 2026.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: Starship IFT-12 Timeline Slips from Late April to May 2026 — Booster 19 Awaits 33-Engine Static Fire. SpaceX scrubbed the Starlink Group 17-35 launch attempt from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base on April 2, 2026, within the 4:03–8:03 p.m.

title: SpaceX History Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: SpaceX closed March 2026 with a historic double-launch day on March 30: Transporter-16 orbited 119 payloads from Vandenberg (booster B1093, 12th flight), crossing the 1,600-total-rideshare-payload milestone, while Starlink 10-44 from Cape Canaveral saw booster B1067 set a world record with its 34th flight — the most flights ever by a single orbital rocket stage. The two March 30 launches pushed SpaceX's 2026 total to 40 missions and all-time booster recoveries to 593. Starship IFT-12 preparations advanced with Ship 39 completing cryoloading preflight objectives at Starbase; the FAA license window runs April 5–October 5, 2026, with a late-April target pending a full Booster 19 static fire. The SpaceX IPO process is advancing, with investor meetings scheduled for April and Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs confirmed as lead underwriters.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: SpaceX's Starship Ship 39 — the first V3 Starship upper stage — completed multiple cryogenic propellant loading preflight test objectives at Starbase, Boca Chica, on March 31, 2026.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Transporter-16, its 16th dedicated rideshare mission, at 4:02 a.m. SpaceX launched the Starlink Group 10-44 mission at 5:15 p.m.

title: SpaceX History Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched its 38th mission of 2026 on March 26 with Starlink 17-17 from Vandenberg, booster B1081's 23rd flight, achieving its 591st total booster recovery (186th on OCISLY). The week's biggest corporate news came from Gwynne Shotwell's TIME magazine cover story (March 26), which revealed SpaceX filed an FCC application to license up to 1 million AI-powered orbital data center satellites, and that SpaceX is scheduling investor briefings for April ahead of an IPO targeting up to $75 billion — with the combined SpaceX+xAI entity valued at $1.25–1.75 trillion. The Transporter-16 rideshare mission (119 payloads) was scrubbed twice on March 23 and 25 due to winds and a boat in the keep-out zone. Booster B1067's record-setting 34th flight on Starlink 10-44 was delayed multiple times, slipping to late March. KPIs, claims, and political profiles were updated to reflect the xAI merger, IPO developments, and Shotwell's new corporate role.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: SpaceX's planned launch of Starlink Group 10-44 from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station was delayed from its targeted March 27 window.

title: SpaceX History Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: SpaceX crossed a landmark milestone on March 16–17, 2026, when a Falcon 9 Starlink Group 17-24 mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base pushed the active Starlink constellation past 10,000 satellites simultaneously in orbit — less than seven years after the first Starlink launch and now representing roughly two-thirds of all active satellites worldwide. SpaceX continued its extraordinary 2026 launch cadence with Falcon 9 missions on March 19 (29 satellites from Cape Canaveral), March 20 (25 satellites from Vandenberg, booster B1100's 4th flight), and March 22 (Starlink 10-62, booster B1078's record 27th flight from Cape Canaveral). Meanwhile, Starship Flight 12 — the debut of the upgraded V3 configuration with Raptor 3 engines — was pushed from a March target to approximately April 2026 following a booster anomaly during pressure testing and subsequent FAA licensing for the April 5–October 5, 2026 window.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: SpaceX's second launch attempt for the Transporter-16 rideshare mission was scrubbed when a boat entered the keep-out zone around SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: SpaceX's first launch attempt for the Transporter-16 rideshare mission from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base was scrubbed due to high upper-level winds.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched the Starlink 10-62 mission on a Falcon 9 from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, deploying 29 Starlink satellites.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched its 30th Starlink batch of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 from Space Launch Complex 4 East at Vandenberg Space Force Base.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Thu Mar 19, 2026
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket lifted off from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station at 10:20 a.m.

title: SpaceX History Tracker Update — Mon Mar 16, 2026
summary: SpaceX's Starlink Group 17-24 mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 10:19 p.m.
